objective: Are there finite sets of primes P and Q such that 1 = (sum over p in P of 1/p)(sum over q in Q of 1/q)?
completion_criteria: A positive answer is a pair of finite prime sets checked by exact rational arithmetic.
non_results: Finite computation outside its stated range is not a proof of a universal claim.
A candidate is not a solution until independent and human review are recorded.
Restating a known reduction, checking examples, or giving an unproved key lemma is not terminal success.

baseline_review.requirement: Repeat when the authoritative status changes materially or the baseline is explicitly invalidated.

synthesis_summary: The source audit confirms that Erdős Problem 307 remains open. Exact arithmetic independently reproduced the known 59-prime threshold and both encodings agreed on all 57002 assignments in the bounded control shell, with no solution there. A dependency-free exact certificate verifier rejected four adversarial controls. The finite numerical input behind the reported side-product barrier was checked exactly. The universal Lean barrier was not locally reproduced: the first build stopped at ENOSPC and the single post-repair retry stopped during mathlib cache retrieval with failed to create thread under a 4096 MB limit, before theorem compilation.

established_facts.claim: Every solution satisfies A(P)=M(Q), A(Q)=M(P), and P intersect Q is empty.
established_facts.evidence: Elementary reduced-fraction proof in notes/baseline-audit-2026-07-21.md, consistent with Bado 2026 and Bonfioli v1.0.0.
established_facts.scope: All finite sets of primes P,Q.

established_facts.claim: Every solution has at least 59 primes in P union Q.
established_facts.evidence: AM-GM, distinct-prime reciprocal extremality, and two independent exact computations of the 58/59 boundary.
established_facts.scope: All finite prime-set solutions; this is an established correction already public in 2019, not a new result.

established_facts.claim: No solution exists in the first-ten-prime assignment shell.
established_facts.evidence: Exhaustive enumeration and independent payload checker in experiments 20260721-004424-f76e4e and 20260721-004437-ff8f64.
established_facts.scope: Exactly all 57002 assignments of the first ten primes to neither/P/Q with P,Q nonempty.

established_facts.claim: The ceiling of the exact finite barrier ratio's square root is 209298852061282362260655116218815858656815144494074532631.
established_facts.evidence: .proof-experiments/20260721-005307-efbf19 checks this integer and its predecessor by exact arithmetic.
established_facts.scope: Finite numerical input only; it does not independently establish the universal barrier.

strategies.family: counterexample and witness search with formal barrier audit
strategies.title: Mandatory source-and-method baseline, exact structural reduction, bounded formulation calibration, fail-closed certificate verification, and attempted reproduction of the strongest reported Lean barrier
strategies.mechanism: Reduce every candidate to exact integer forcing equations, validate a fixed finite shell under independent encodings, provide a fail-closed verifier, and audit the pinned formal lower-bound source before permitting search
strategies.hypothesis: The exact smallest-prime reciprocal threshold is 59, the rational identity agrees with the forced integer equations throughout the first-ten-prime shell, and the tagged Bonfioli barrier can be independently rebuilt with its claimed axiom footprint.
strategies.discriminating_test: Compute the 58/59 threshold with exact fractions, exhaust all 57002 nonempty disjoint assignments of the first ten primes under two encodings, and run the clean tagged Lean source through lake build and Check.lean.
strategies.blocker: No construction family is known that generates barrier-scale squarefree arithmetic-derivative two-cycles. Independently reproducing the strongest reported Lean barrier is additionally blocked by mathlib cache retrieval failing to create a thread under the prior 4096 MB experiment limit.
strategies.reopen_condition: For the formal audit, provide memory above 4096 MB or a documented supported serialization setting. For frontier search, provide a barrier-scale exact construction family with measurable squarefree forced-partner output.

strategies.mechanism: Finish the pinned kernel build and inspect the exact axiom footprints of card_ge_59 and erdos307_barrier_closed.
strategies.hypothesis: The tagged closed barrier compiles without sorryAx and uses only the documented axioms and native_decide.
strategies.discriminating_test: A clean lake build followed by Check.lean output under adequate or serialized memory.
strategies.rationale: This has higher information value than any bounded candidate sweep below the reported barrier.
strategies.title: Finish the pinned kernel build and inspect the exact axiom footprints of card_ge_59 and erdos307_barrier_closed.

strategies.mechanism: Replace trial-division primality with certificate-carrying Pratt or Pocklington verification while retaining exact forced-partner closure.
strategies.hypothesis: A compact deterministic certificate schema can verify future large prime factors without a probable-prime trust gap.
strategies.discriminating_test: One known large-prime certificate must pass two implementations and every mutated factor or witness must fail.
strategies.rationale: It strengthens the verification surface before expensive generation and remains useful even without a candidate.
strategies.title: Replace trial-division primality with certificate-carrying Pratt or Pocklington verification while retaining exact forced-partner closure.

open_leads.description: Complete the pinned Lean barrier and axiom audit under adequate or serialized memory.
open_leads.rationale: This determines whether the strongest current exclusion can be promoted from externally reported to locally kernel-reproduced.
open_leads.next_experiment: Run scripts/check_bonfioli_barrier.sh once through the experiment recorder with memory above 4096 MB or a documented supported single-thread cache configuration.

open_leads.description: Add proof-carrying primality to the exact certificate format.
open_leads.rationale: Trial division is exact but infeasible for the enormous primes expected in any witness.
open_leads.next_experiment: Define a Pratt or Pocklington JSON schema, accept one known certified prime under two implementations, and reject every one-field mutation.

open_leads.description: Test construction families solely through squarefree arithmetic-derivative closure.
open_leads.rationale: A candidate is determined by one squarefree a with squarefree a' and a''=a; independent P,Q generation is unnecessary.
open_leads.next_experiment: Specify a family reaching a greater than 2e56, evaluate exact squarefreeness and closure on a fixed small parameter sample, and stop if it cannot reach the scale or only optimizes decimal proximity.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Independent two-sided subset search or floating-point genetic optimization as a proof-bearing route.
ruled_out.reason: Q is forced by A(P), while decimal proximity does not certify either integer forcing equality.
ruled_out.evidence: Reduced-fraction forcing theorem, exact verifier, and comparison with the reported Rubbens search.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: An exact theorem or construction showing that the proxy preserves or efficiently predicts forced-partner closure.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Reporting 60 as the sharp consequence of the elementary reciprocal-sum argument.
ruled_out.scope: All future statements of that argument.
ruled_out.reason: The exact crossing occurs at the 59th prime.
ruled_out.evidence: Independent exact computations and the 2019 MathOverflow correction.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Another arbitrary bounded search beneath the reported 2e56 side-product scale.
ruled_out.scope: A claimed contribution or solution search relying on Bonfioli's reported theorem.
ruled_out.reason: It would repeat a range already excluded by the closest source and would not improve its result.
ruled_out.evidence: Bonfioli v1.0.0 source and Formal Conjectures annotation; local kernel reproduction remains pending.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: The formal barrier fails an independent kernel audit, or the computation targets a separately motivated structural statistic with an explicit acceptance path.

next_session.objective: Independently reproduce or precisely reject the Bonfioli v1.0.0 universal barrier, then harden certificate verification.
next_session.first_action: Reuse /root/.cache/proof-factory/erdos307-v1.0.0 and invoke scripts/check_bonfioli_barrier.sh once through run_experiment.py with memory above 4096 MB, or first document a supported single-thread setting; do not reclone.
next_session.stop_condition: Stop after one successful lake build plus Check.lean axiom audit, or the first new precisely diagnosed failure. If successful, proceed only to one proof-carrying primality control.
